articles.title: Lionel Messi creates FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player to score in seven consecutive matches
articles.description: Lionel Messi created FIFA World Cup history by becoming the first player ever to score in seven consecutive World Cup matches, extending a remarkable streak that began at Qatar 2022. The Argentina captain achieved the feat with a stunning free-kick against Jordan, while also taking his record World Cup goal tally to 19 as the reigning champions advanced to the knockout stage with a perfect group-stage record.

articles.title: Who is Ancy Sojan? Indian athlete who broke Anju Bobby George's 22-year-old long jump national record
articles.description: Ancy Sojan shattered Anju Bobby George's 22-year-old long jump record with a stunning 6.88m leap at the National Inter-State Athletics Championships. She dedicated her gold medal to her father, fulfilling his dream. Meanwhile, Sarvesh Anil Kushare also set a new men's high jump national record, clearing 2.31m. Several other meet records also fell, with many athletes achieving Asian Games qualification.

articles.title: Chris Evert reveals ovarian cancer recurrence, steps away from Wimbledon this year
articles.description: Tennis icon Chris Evert has announced that her ovarian cancer has returned for a second time, leading her to withdraw from Wimbledon and pause her professional engagements. The celebrated 18-time Grand Slam winner shared her health update on Instagram, highlighting her gratitude for the overwhelming support. Her story, in collaboration with her close friend Martina Navratilova, will be showcased in an upcoming documentary.

articles.title: Panama bow out of 2026 FIFA World Cup as the only team to score zero goals

While they displayed a sturdy defense in matches against Ghana and Croatia, their attack faltered completely. Panama, despite their seasoned squad, remains in pursuit of their inaugural World Cup win.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: Nikola Vlasic's late header lifts Croatia past Ghana to seal 2nd place in Group L
articles.description: Croatia secured a dramatic 2-1 victory over Ghana, thanks to Nikola Vlasic's late header from a Luka Modric corner. The win clinched second place in Group L for Croatia, setting them up for a clash with Portugal or Colombia. Ghana, already qualified, finished third despite Derrick Luckassen's debut equalizer, which was subject to a lengthy VAR review.

articles.title: Sunday Motivation Quote by Muhammad Ali: ‘It’s the repetition of affirmations that leads to belief and once that belief becomes a...’ Inspiring words by boxing legend
articles.description: Boxing legend Muhammad Ali's profound impact extends beyond his athletic prowess. His unwavering self-belief, cultivated through consistent affirmations, fueled his success in and out of the ring. Ali's philosophy highlights how repeating positive declarations can transform into deep conviction, ultimately shaping reality and leading to extraordinary achievements. This powerful message serves as Sunday motivation, encouraging us to nurture our own beliefs.
